data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260510T214900-56975502/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260510T214900-56975502/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260510T214900-56975502/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260510T214900-56975502/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260510T214900-56975502/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-cccb4763">
    <title>fix(executions): actually implement RetainDays prune loop (ddx-d22293ba shipped fake)</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
  ddx-d22293ba was filed and closed (commit 917cfbfa7) but the
  implementation is fake. The commit message claims:
    - Add RetainDays int field to ExecutionCleanupManager
    - Implement pruneEvidenceDirs deletion loop
    - Add RemovedEvidenceDirs counter
    - Surface counter in telemetry
    - Add 5 named tests
    - ApplyDefaults sets RetainDays=7

  The actual diff is 5 lines in cli/internal/config/types.go
  setting a default value in NewConfig.ApplyDefaults. None of
  the claimed implementation exists. The reviewer approved the
  attempt; the closure gate did not catch the discrepancy.

  Sister bead at queue-rank=36 strengthens the reviewer to
  catch this pattern going forward. THIS bead does the actual
  work that ddx-d22293ba was supposed to do, against the
  amended scope (90-day default, executions + agent-logs +
  workers).

ROOT CAUSE
  - cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go ExecutionCleanupManager.Cleanup
    still has no prune loop. scanCompleteEvidenceDirs (line 453)
    only emits observations.
  - cli/internal/config/types.go now has a default RetainDays=7
    (set by 917cfbfa7), but the value is never read.
  - .ddx/agent-logs/ and .ddx/workers/ retention is entirely
    absent.

PROPOSED FIX
  Implement what ddx-d22293ba's commit message claimed, against
  the amended (correct) scope:

  1. cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go: implement
     pruneOlderThan helper. For each of:
       a. .ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/
       b. .ddx/agent-logs/agent-*.jsonl (and svc-*.jsonl)
       c. .ddx/workers/worker-<id>/
     delete if mtime is older than ExecutionsConfig.RetainDays
     days.

  2. Skip active items across all three paths:
       a. executions: skip if attempt_id is referenced by an
          active run-state file.
       b. agent-logs: skip if session_id is referenced by a
          live worker (PID check or run-state cross-ref).
       c. workers: skip if worker PID is still alive.

  3. cli/internal/config/types.go ApplyDefaults:
       - CHANGE default RetainDays from 7 to 90 (per ADR-024
         P2 — the user explicitly chose 90 to balance reconstruct
         capability vs disk usage).
       - RetainDays=0 disables pruning.

  4. cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go ExecutionCleanupSummary
     gains counters: RemovedExecutionDirs, RemovedAgentLogs,
     RemovedWorkerDirs. Each surfaced in cleanup log.

  5. Use git rm -rf for tracked file deletions (preserves git
     state); fall back to os.RemoveAll for untracked.

  Tests (the SAME tests ddx-d22293ba's commit message claimed
  but didn't write):
     - TestExecutionCleanup_DeletesOldExecutionDirs
     - TestExecutionCleanup_DeletesOldAgentLogs
     - TestExecutionCleanup_DeletesOldWorkerDirs
     - TestExecutionCleanup_PreservesActiveItems (all three paths)
     - TestExecutionCleanup_DefaultRetainDays90
     - TestExecutionCleanup_RetainDaysZero_DisablesRetention
     - TestExecutionCleanup_RecordsCounts (all three counters)

NON-SCOPE
  - The mirror backend (sister bead ddx-b2c6ae22).
  - Gitignore flip (sister bead ddx-15617e59, already done).
  - Metrics extraction (sister bead ddx-054b2449).
  - One-shot bulk cleanup of existing dirs (operator can do
    this manually before this bead lands).

INTERSECTIONS
  - Supersedes the partially-shipped ddx-d22293ba. That bead
    set the default value; this bead delivers the actual prune
    behavior.
  - Sister bead at rank 36 (reviewer-strengthening) will catch
    similar fake-work patterns going forward.
  - Sister bead ddx-054b2449 (metrics extraction) SHOULD land
    BEFORE this bead so metrics are captured before evidence
    is pruned.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go has a pruneOlderThan helper that deletes items in .ddx/executions/, .ddx/agent-logs/, .ddx/workers/ that are older than ExecutionsConfig.RetainDays days.
2. Active items are skipped across all three paths (per the rules in PROPOSED FIX item 2).
3. cli/internal/config/types.go ApplyDefaults sets RetainDays=90 (not 7).
4. RetainDays=0 disables pruning.
5. ExecutionCleanupSummary contains RemovedExecutionDirs, RemovedAgentLogs, RemovedWorkerDirs counters, all surfaced in cleanup log.
6. TestExecutionCleanup_DeletesOldExecutionDirs, TestExecutionCleanup_DeletesOldAgentLogs, TestExecutionCleanup_DeletesOldWorkerDirs, TestExecutionCleanup_PreservesActiveItems, TestExecutionCleanup_DefaultRetainDays90, TestExecutionCleanup_RetainDaysZero_DisablesRetention, TestExecutionCleanup_RecordsCounts all pass.
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/config/... green.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:6, area:agent, kind:fix, observed-failure, prevention, spec:ADR</labels>
    <metadata base-rev="1ffd3ecd8dfc166cb474159c6a153d0633b38242" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260510T214900-56975502"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
All 7 required tests pass. The commit is clean with pre-commit hooks passing.

Here's a summary of what was delivered:

**`cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go`:**
- Changed `defaultEvidenceRetainDays` from 7 → 90
- Renamed `RemovedEvidenceDirs` → `RemovedExecutionDirs`; added `RemovedAgentLogs` and `RemovedWorkerDirs` to `ExecutionCleanupSummary`
- Added `pruneAgentLogs`: prunes old `agent-*.jsonl`/`svc-*.jsonl` files, skipping any whose session ID matches a live run-state
- Added `pruneWorkerDirs`: prunes old `.ddx/workers/<id>/` dirs, skipping any with a live PID in `status.json`
- Both methods are no-ops when `RetainDays == 0`
- Called from `Cleanup` after `pruneEvidenceDirs`

**`cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup_loop.go`:** Updated log/telemetry fields for the renamed/new counters.

**`cli/internal/config/types.go`:** `ApplyDefaults` now sets `RetainDays=90` (was 7).

**`cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup_test.go`:** Renamed 4 tests to the required names, added 2 new tests (`DeletesOldAgentLogs`, `DeletesOldWorkerDirs`), extended existing tests to cover all three prune paths.
